title: Skill evaluation schema
description: Metaschema for per-skill eval YAML files. Each eval declares one scenario (input + expected behaviour) the runtime can execute against any LLM and report pass/fail uniformly. Assertion grammar is restricted by design — see assertion_grammar below.

name.description: Unique within the skill. Convention: eval-NN-short-slug.

skill.description: Skill id matching the parent skill.manifest.json

description.description: One-line summary of what this eval verifies

category.description: Eval-coverage-matrix bucket. happy_path / edge_case / compliance_failure / cross_validation are the canonical four. skill_specific is the additional-coverage bucket. validation_trap / rationale_block / jurisdiction_switch / missing_input are emerged sub-categories.

input.description: Inline input dict mapping inputs.json item ids → answers. Validated by the skill's inputs metaschema at runtime. Mutually exclusive with input_fixture.

input_fixture.description: Path string pointing at an example input.json (e.g. '../examples/uk-domestic/input.json'). Singular. May be null when test_scenario carries the full input override. Mutually exclusive with inline input.

input_fixtures.description: References to example folders that serve as inputs for this eval. Mutually exclusive with input.

expected_output_fixture.description: Path string pointing at the expected output.json for fixture-based evals. Singular form used by most fixture evals.

expected_output_fixtures.description: Plural variant: multiple expected outputs for fan-out evals that run the skill against several fixtures and compare each.

test_scenario.description: Optional scenario-specific overrides applied to the loaded input_fixture (e.g. force a smaller breaker rating to create a compliance trap). Free-form; runtime merges this on top of the fixture input.

expected_compliance.description: Optional declarations of the compliance outcome the skill should report.

expected_flags.description: Substrings the runtime expects to find in ir.flags or ir.rationale flags.

assertion_grammar.note: Assertion grammar is restricted by design. To extend (new operator, function call, arithmetic), raise an issue first — runtime parsers must stay simple.

assertion.description: Single line: jsonpath + operator + value. See assertion_grammar above. Example: 'ir.luminaires.length >= 16'.

severity.description: critical = fail eval; warning = report but pass; info = report only

matches_inv.description: Cross-reference to an INV-N invariant id (e.g. 'INV-3'). Lets evals declare which invariant they verify.

matches_nec.description: Cross-reference to an NEC article (e.g. 'Article 210.8' or 'NEC 250.122'). Used in US-jurisdiction evals.
